profile_raw:
schema_version: 1
kind: media_stack_profile
metadata:
  name: media-stack
  platform: k8s
  purpose: standard
resources:
  disk_space_gb: 500
  network_cidr: 192.168.1.0/24
install_profile: standard
bootstrap:
  preconfigure_apps: true
  preconfigure_api_keys: true
  apply_initial_preferences: true
  auto_download_content: false
  trigger_indexer_sync: true
  refresh_health_after_setup: true
routing:
  internet_exposed: false
  strategy: hybrid
  provider: envoy
  base_domain: local
  gateway_port: 80
  app_path_prefix: /app
  media_server_service_names:
  - jellyfin
  - jellyfin-nvidia
  gateway_host: k8.media-stack.local
  direct_hosts:
    media_server: jellyfin.k8.media-stack.local
# Auth defaults to Authelia on K8s — clusters are typically reachable
# via real DNS even when ``internet_exposed: false`` (the operator's
# router exposes ports, family members access from outside the LAN,
# etc.). A ``provider: none`` default would mean every fresh K8s
# install is wide open by default, which is exactly the v1.0.166
# incident the security promises now catch. Operators who want a
# pure-LAN no-auth setup can override this in their own profile.
auth:
  enabled: true
  provider: authelia
chaos:
  enabled: false
  duration_minutes: 5
  interval_seconds: 60
  actions:
  - restart_container
  - pause_container
  - network_disconnect
live_tv_defaults:
  tuner_url: https://iptv-org.github.io/iptv/countries/us.m3u
  guide_url: https://iptv-epg.org/files/epg-us.xml
  default_program_icon_urls:
  - https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iptv-org/logo/master/tv.png
technology_bindings:
  torrent_client: qbittorrent
  usenet_client: sabnzbd
  media_server: jellyfin
  request_manager: jellyseerr
  indexer_manager: prowlarr
app_auth:
  enabled: true
  method: Forms
  required: DisabledForLocalAddresses
  fail_on_error: false
  username_env: STACK_ADMIN_USERNAME
  password_env: STACK_ADMIN_PASSWORD
